decisions.briefing: Market is risk-off today with NASDAQ down 1.1% and S&P 500 down 0.3%, and the screener returned no candidates. Following the established cadence of holding through low-conviction days and letting stop-losses and profit targets manage existing positions automatically — no action taken.

market_education.summary_en: Today's risk-off session saw the NASDAQ fall 1.1% while the broader S&P 500 shed 0.3%, reflecting a rotation out of growth and technology names. Risk-off moves of this magnitude typically signal that institutional investors are reducing exposure to higher-beta assets and moving toward defensives or cash. Without a clear catalyst to reverse the selling pressure, the prudent posture is to hold existing positions and wait for market conditions to stabilize before deploying fresh capital.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場呈現避險情緒，那斯達克指數下跌1.1%，標普500指數跌0.3%，顯示資金正從成長型及科技股撤出，轉向防禦性資產或現金。此類風險趨避走勢通常反映機構投資者正在降低高Beta值部位的風險敞口。在缺乏明確反彈催化劑的情況下，審慎策略是持有現有倉位，靜待市況趨穩後再部署新資金。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' day is when investors broadly move away from riskier assets like tech stocks and toward safer ones like bonds or cash. It often shows up as the NASDAQ falling more than the S&P 500, since NASDAQ is packed with high-growth companies that investors are quickest to sell when uncertainty rises. On risk-off days, the smartest move is usually to hold existing positions and not add new ones.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指投資者普遍撤離科技股等風險較高的資產，轉向債券或現金等避風港的市場狀態。這種情況通常表現為那斯達克跌幅大於標普500，因為那斯達克集中了大量高成長企業，市場不確定性升溫時這類股票往往最先遭到拋售。在避險日，最穩健的策略通常是持有現有部位，暫緩建立新倉。
